purpose: the reference hand-built Oct Tree Distribution — a CUSTOM artist-authored Scene Nodes graph that ships as a Distribution generator (plugin 190000011) and feeds the new C4D 2026 advanced Cloner mode. Demonstrates the WHOLE PIPELINE of authoring custom procedural scatter algorithms for the new Distribution → Cloner workflow.

[1028].name: Clones from Distribution
[1028].purpose: Toggle whether Cloner reads from a Distribution generator at all

[2115].name: Distribution
[2115].purpose: BaseObject link to a Distribution generator

[5511].name: Display
[5511].purpose: Viewport debug overlay for distribution data

stock_distribution_internals_finding: Stock distributions ship as VERSIONED SCENE NODES ASSETS (Asset Version 1002, descid path '777.1852142638.1835104367.1848536687.1684352610.1634952494.1634956133.1953916274.1936289646.1') with ~190 AM-exposed descids each. The exposed params are deeply-nested floatingio paths (e.g. Cannonball 'Selected Objects' at '777.1768833104.1901617015.1194414149.1748332115.1500673399.1732659790.1946157056') and are NOT Python-settable per the FIO/PORTLIST gotcha — they require UI interaction to wire input geometry.

octree_custom_internals.am_exposure: Minimal — only 1 'Distribution' descid; the artist did not add FloatingIO exposure for parameter control. Input geometry hardcoded in graph rather than exposed as AM link.
octree_custom_internals.complexity_vs_stocks: 65 nodes (smallest in the family). Smart Hex 210, Projected 2D Grid 221, Cannonball 251, Spline Bead 653, Partition 1268. Oct Tree is the IDEAL study size for understanding the pattern.

experiments_run.result: Cloner shows 7 child shapes scattered randomly (Object mode, no target)
Beautiful HIERARCHICAL OCTREE PATTERN of clustered spheres with finer subdivisions in denser regions
EMPTY VIEWPORT — Cannonball has 'Selected Objects' link unset (not Python-settable due to deep floatingio path)
KITBASH/MECHA effect — partitioned regions, each region gets a different child shape (Sphere/Tube/Atom Array/Cube)
experiments_run.key_insight: Partition is the 'kitbash distribution' — perfect for greebles, instrument panels, mechanical assemblages

experiments_run.result: DENSE HEX GRID of varied shapes — zoomed massively (camera framing inadequate)

blockers_observed: Stock distributions (Cannonball, Projected 2D Grid, Spline Bead) all rendered EMPTY because their 'Selected Objects' / source-input links are deeply-nested floatingio params (root 777.<long-path>) that cannot be set via Python — UI interaction required
Partition + Smart Hex worked because they have valid defaults that don't require explicit input wiring
Smart Hex camera framing was wildly inadequate — generates clones at scale that engulfs StudyCam

recipe_candidates.title: Author a custom Distribution generator (artist-shippable scatter algorithm)
recipe_candidates.priority: HIGH — unblocks shipping any procedural scatter as a Cloner-compatible tool
ingredients: Scene Nodes graph hosted in plugin 190000011 (Distribution)
Standard Cloner-facing input ports: clonerchildrenin, globalclonermatrixin, localclonermatrixin
Standard time inputs: time, frame, fps, nimbus, globalmatin
Custom algorithm core (octree / blue-noise / curvature-driven / RD / whatever)
Output: composedistributioncontainer node terminating in distdataout port
recipe_candidates.reference_implementation: Oct Tree Distribution by the reference build — 65 nodes, demonstrates octree spatial subdivision + closestpointonsurface for surface-aware scatter. Bare-graph style (no AM exposure).
recipe_candidates.next_step_for_artist_ship: Add FloatingIO ports to surface controls (count, density, scale, seed, source-object) for AM exposure — follow the stock distributions' versioned-asset pattern (Asset Version 1002 at 777.<path>)

tooling_action_items_for_cinema4d_mcp: Add plugin 190000011 to dissect_capsule scan + asset registry
Crawl scene_nodes_list_assets(source='repository', filter_substring='distribution') to identify the 5 stock asset IDs
Add a deep-floatingio setter helper that knows how to traverse 777.<long-path> paths via UI commands rather than Python (likely needs a CallCommand or scripted UI action)
Consider adding a 'Distribution sandbox' helper to cinema4d-mcp: create N distributions, plug in a torus, screenshot each — useful for visual reference
Update c4d_2026_api_gotchas.md with the Distribution + Advanced Cloner section
